Package de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy

Class ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy.ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition
All Implemented Interfaces:
StopCondition
Direct Known Subclasses:
SymbolicExecutionBreakpointStopCondition
public class ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition extends Object implements StopCondition

This StopCondition stops the auto mode (ApplyStrategy) if a given number (getMaximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal()) of maximal executed symbolic execution tree nodes is reached in a goal.

If a Node in KeY's proof tree is also a node in a symbolic execution tree is computed via SymbolicExecutionUtil#isSymbolicExecutionTreeNode(Node).

The auto mode is stopped exactly in the open goal Node which will become the next symbolic execution tree node.

    Modifier and Type

    Description
    static final int
    MAXIMAL_NUMBER_OF_SET_NODES_TO_EXECUTE_PER_GOAL_FOR_ONE_STEP
    The default maximal number of steps to do exactly one step in each goal.
    static final int
    MAXIMAL_NUMBER_OF_SET_NODES_TO_EXECUTE_PER_GOAL_IN_COMPLETE_RUN
    The default maximal number of steps to simulate a complete program execution.

    • ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition

      public ExecutedSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition(int maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal)
      Constructor to stop after the given number of symbolic execution tree nodes.
      Parameters:
      maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal - The maximal number of allowed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal.

  • Method Details

    • getMaximalWork

      public int getMaximalWork(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof)
      Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar. Pass 0 to indicate unknown size.
      Specified by:
      getMaximalWork in interface StopCondition
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
      proof - The current Proof.
      Returns:
      The maximal amount of work or 0 if it is unknown.

    • isGoalAllowed

      public boolean isGoalAllowed(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal)
      Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected Goal chosen by the GoalChooser before it is applied. If it is not allowed the apply strategy will stop.
      Specified by:
      isGoalAllowed in interface StopCondition
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
      proof - The current Proof.
      startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
      Returns:
      true rule application is allowed, false rule application is not allowed so stop apply strategy

    • handleNodeLimitExceeded

      protected void handleNodeLimitExceeded(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal, Node node, RuleApp ruleApp, Integer executedNumberOfSetNodes)
      Handles the state that the node limit is exceeded.
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
      proof - The current Proof.
      startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.nanoTime()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
      node - The Node of the current Goal.
      ruleApp - The current RuleApp.
      executedNumberOfSetNodes - The executed number of SET nodes.

    • handleNodeLimitNotExceeded

      protected void handleNodeLimitNotExceeded(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal, Node node, RuleApp ruleApp, Integer executedNumberOfSetNodes)
      Handles the state that the node limit is not exceeded.
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
      proof - The current Proof.
      startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.nanoTime()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
      node - The Node of the current Goal.
      ruleApp - The current RuleApp.
      executedNumberOfSetNodes - The executed number of SET nodes.

    • getGoalNotAllowedMessage

      public String getGoalNotAllowedMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal)
      Returns the reason why the previous check via #isGoalAllowed(ApplyStrategy, int, long, Proof, GoalChooser, long, int, Goal) has stopped the apply strategy.
      Specified by:
      getGoalNotAllowedMessage in interface StopCondition
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
      proof - The current Proof.
      startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
      Returns:

    • shouldStop

      public boolean shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, long startTime, int countApplied, SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.
      Specified by:
      shouldStop in interface StopCondition
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
      proof - The current Proof.
      startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      true stop strategy, false continue strategy and apply next rule.

    • getStopMessage

      public String getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, long startTime, int countApplied, SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Returns a human readable message which explains why the previous #shouldStop(ApplyStrategy, Proof, GoalChooser, long, int, SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
      Specified by:
      getStopMessage in interface StopCondition
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
      proof - The current Proof.
      startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      The human readable message which explains the stop reason.

    • getMaximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal

      public int getMaximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecutePerGoal()
      Returns the maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal per auto mode run.
      Returns:
      The maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal per auto mode run.

      Sets the maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per goal per auto mode run.
      Parameters:
      maximalNumberOfSetNodesToExecute - The maximal number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per per goal auto mode run.

    • wasSetNodeExecuted

      public boolean wasSetNodeExecuted()
      Checks if at least one symbolic execution tree node was executed.
      Returns:
      true at least one symbolic execution tree node was executed, false no symbolic execution tree node was executed.

    • getExectuedSetNodesPerGoal

      Returns the number of executed symbolic execution tree nodes per Goal.
      Returns:
